business.geography_hint: EU (EU labor market, GDPR, EU Pay Transparency Directive 2023/970)

engine.ladder_reasoning: Visitor is evaluating a THING — two productized 'Intel' reads (Role Intel, Profile Intel) plus an in-development Capability Index — via features, deliverables ('What you get'), methodology and sample outputs. It is a product/tool being assessed, not a person being trusted or work being browsed.

goal.one_action: join the private-beta waitlist / request early access

structure.archetype_note: Multi-page productized-SaaS site: home overview + per-product deep pages (Role Intel / Profile Intel), a B2B/teams page carrying pricing, a methodology credibility page, and support/resources. Converges on a single external waitlist form.

mapped.observed_as: Job titles say less every year.
Role Intel / Profile Intel / Role × Profile
When to Use It / Who it's for / Best for
No dashboard. No onboarding. Just the read. / How a ReLoop'd Read is produced
The method behind the read / Why it holds
Two forces collided / The starting point / Why now
Isn't this just AI prompting? / What IT IS — WHAT IT ISN'T / Transparency boundary
Signal Interpretations Table (market assumption vs ReLoop interpretation)
Reads the job €7 / Reads the candidate €17 (on /b2b)
Who's Behind It — Ligia Adam bio
EU-built. Human-calibrated. Structured data your team owns. (GDPR)

unmapped.observed: Sample preview: PI-2207 / Sample preview (Head of Digital) — a shortened example of the actual report deliverable
unmapped.why_no_fit: Canonical list has no 'example-output / sample deliverable' section; it is neither results (real customer outcomes) nor features (it IS the product's output rendered)

unmapped.observed: Current State · Private Beta — status roadmap (LIVE/BETA vs in-development per module)
unmapped.why_no_fit: No canonical section expresses a product-status/roadmap matrix; it's a maturity ledger, not features or results

proposed_new.name: external-list-signup
proposed_new.evidence: Every primary conversion CTA ('Join the waitlist', 'Join the private beta') routes to off-site Tally forms (tally.so/r/aQkONB, tally.so/r/Ek81zA) rather than an on-page form; list/beta capture is delegated off-site.

confidence.why: fetch_quality=full with all 6 crawled pages, nav, CTAs, forms and pricing captured; engine and goal are unambiguous. Tally form fields not inspectable, but that does not affect classification.

gap_findings: Pre-launch/beta product with a mix of LIVE and 'in-development' modules — the taxonomy has no way to express a product-status/roadmap section (Current State · Private Beta).

'Sample preview' of the actual deliverable (an example report) is a distinct proof mechanism — the productized output shown as proof — that maps to neither features, results, nor testimonials cleanly.

Pricing (€7/€17) lives only on the /b2b page while the individual product pages omit it — 'packages' capability assumes pricing co-located with the offer; split-audience pricing (self-serve vs teams) isn't expressible.
Waitlist/beta conversion is entirely delegated to external Tally forms (external-list-signup), so on-page lead-form capability under-describes the real handoff.
